sections.instructions: This section has a printed list of medication names. The patient circles the ones they take. ONLY include a medication if its name is clearly INSIDE a circle — if a circle merely touches or overlaps an adjacent name, that adjacent name is NOT circled. Patients may also write additional medication names by hand near or below the printed list. There is also a 'Medications For:' line at the bottom listing condition categories.

fields.field_label: Please circle medications you are taking?
fields.extraction_hints: CRITICAL: Only report a medication as circled if the printed medication NAME is clearly INSIDE the circle. If a circle is drawn around one word and merely touches or overlaps an adjacent word, the adjacent word is NOT circled — only the word the circle is drawn around counts. Circles can be any color. If no medications are circled, return empty. Do NOT confuse handwritten text near the list with circled printed names — handwritten additions go in p1_additional_medications_written instead.

Look for handwritten medication names that the patient added NEXT TO, ABOVE, or BELOW the printed medication list. These are medications NOT in the printed list that the patient wrote in by hand. Common examples: Lyrica, Duloxetine, Hydrocod/Acetam, Nortex, etc. Transcribe all handwritten medication names as a comma-separated list. If handwritten text appears BELOW the 'Medications For:' line, it belongs to p1_additional_conditions_written instead.

Bottom of the medication section. 'Medications For:' followed by condition categories. The patient circles or checks which conditions their medications treat. Only report conditions that are clearly marked.

Look for handwritten condition names or notes written BELOW the 'Medications For:' line. These are conditions NOT in the printed list that the patient added by hand. Transcribe as a comma-separated list.

standalone_tables.table_title: BELOW THE LINE TO BE COMPLETED BY THE DOCTOR:
standalone_tables.extraction_hints: This is a grid table completed by the doctor. Each cell can contain an X, checkmark, arrow, or other mark — any mark means TRUE for that row+column intersection. The POSITION of the mark determines which condition (row) and which status (column) it applies to. For example: an X in the 'Gastric Acid' row under 'DX Pre-Injury' means gastric acid was diagnosed pre-injury. An arrow or checkmark in 'Increased After Injury' means the condition worsened after the industrial injury. Empty cells mean FALSE/not applicable. Check each of the 5 rows x 3 columns (15 cells total). The mark can be any color.

pages.notes: Form contains conditional logic where sub-questions appear based on YES answers to main questions. Medical history section has numbered questions 1-17. Table at bottom is for doctor completion only.

sections.instructions: Section title: "What medications were you taking before the work injury?" The first line has two checkable options: "The patient cannot remember at this time" (left) and "None" (right). If either has an X or mark, all category lines below will be blank. Below that are 9 category lines (Pain, Inflammation, Stress, Sleep, Gastric Reflux, Diabetes, High Blood Pressure, Thyroid Problem, Other) each with a horizontal line where the patient writes medication names. Each medication category has a printed label on the LEFT (e.g. "Pain", "Inflammation", "Stress") followed by a horizontal line. Handwritten medication names appear ON that same horizontal line, to the RIGHT of the label. Attribute handwritten text to whichever printed label is on the SAME horizontal line. If text is slightly above or below a line, associate it with the nearest category label.

standalone_fields.position_description: bottom of page, next to signature

pages.notes: Page contains attestation statement 'I attest that all of the above is true.' which is static text, not an input field. Several conditional follow-up questions depend on yes/no answers from parent fields.

sections.instructions: Top of page — a row of office location names printed in all caps. The patient or staff circles ONE location. Only the name clearly INSIDE the circle is selected.

fields.position_description: first row at very top of page
fields.extraction_hints: Row of office locations printed in all caps across the top of the page. Only ONE should be circled. Only the word clearly INSIDE the circle counts — if the circle touches an adjacent word, that word is NOT selected. The circle can be any color.

sections.instructions: Second row below office location — a row of evaluation type abbreviations. The patient or staff circles ONE type. Only the abbreviation clearly INSIDE the circle is selected.

fields.position_description: second row, below office location row

sections.position_description: top of page, below office location

sections.instructions: Row of demographic fields below the office location and evaluation type rows. Layout: 'Name: ___' then 'M___ F___' then 'Date: ___/___/202___' then 'Interpreter: ___' all on the same line. Below that: 'Date of Injury: ___' then 'PTP: DR___' on the next line. Each field label is printed and the handwritten answer appears on the horizontal line to the RIGHT of that label.

fields.extraction_hints: Handwritten patient name on the line after 'Name:'. This is on the third row of the page (below office location and evaluation type). The name appears to the LEFT on this row, before 'M_F_'.

'M' and 'F' appear on the same row as 'Name:', to the RIGHT of the name. The patient circles ONE — either M or F. Only the letter clearly INSIDE the circle is the answer. The circle can be any color. If neither is circled, return null.
Handwritten date on the same row as Name and M/F, to the RIGHT. Format is ___/___/202___ (MM/DD/YYYY). The '202' is pre-printed — the patient fills in the month, day, and last digit(s) of the year.
Handwritten interpreter name on the same row as Name/Date, at the far RIGHT. After 'Interpreter:'. If no interpreter was used, this line will be blank — return null.
Handwritten date on the SECOND row (below the Name/M/F/Date row). After 'Date of Injury:'. This is on the LEFT side of this row.
Handwritten doctor name on the SECOND row, to the RIGHT of 'Date of Injury'. After 'PTP: DR'. PTP = Primary Treating Physician.

sections.position_description: below the evaluation type row, above employment history

sections.instructions: Employment History section. Layout is a structured form with printed labels and horizontal lines. Row 1: 'Employed at: ___' (employer name) then 'for ___ years ___ months' on the same line. Row 2: 'Job Title: ___' — handwritten job title on the line, may overflow with additional text to the right describing the role. Row 3: 'Worked ___ Days per week;' on the LEFT, then 'Worked ___ Hours per day' on the RIGHT of the same line. Row 4: 'Right Left Hand Dominant' — patient circles one. Row 5: 'What did patient do at the job?' — the answer is handwritten BELOW this question on the following line(s), not to the right. The handwritten text may span multiple lines. All text below this question and above the next printed section belongs to this field.

fields.extraction_hints: Handwritten employer name on the line after 'Employed at:'. This is on Row 1 of the employment section, on the LEFT side. The employer name may be long and take up most of the line. Do NOT confuse with the 'for ___ years ___ months' that appears to the RIGHT on the same line.
Handwritten number on Row 1, to the RIGHT of the employer name, after 'for' and before 'years'. This is the number of years employed. May be blank if only months are filled in.
Handwritten number on Row 1, at the far RIGHT, before 'months'. This is the number of months employed. May be blank if only years are filled in.

Handwritten job title on the line after 'Job Title:' on Row 2. The title may be short (e.g. 'OSP', 'Care Giver') or may overflow to the right with additional descriptive text (e.g. 'Direct supervisor, professional, home manager'). Capture ALL handwritten text on this row as the job title — it describes their role.
Handwritten number on Row 3, on the LEFT side, between 'Worked' and 'Days per week;'. Usually a single digit (e.g. 5, 7). May include a range (e.g. '3-5').

Handwritten number on Row 3, on the RIGHT side, between 'Worked' and 'Hours per day'. Usually a number like 8, 10, 24. May also have additional text like 'lbs' or 'week' written nearby — ignore that, only extract the hours number on this line between the printed labels.

Row 4: 'Right' and 'Left' printed side by side, followed by 'Hand Dominant'. The patient circles ONE — either Right or Left. Only the word INSIDE the circle counts. If the circle touches the other word, that word is NOT selected. Circle can be any color.

fields.field_label: What did patient do at the job?
fields.extraction_hints: IMPORTANT: The answer to 'What did patient do at the job?' is written BELOW the question, or to the right of it. Look for handwritten text on the line(s) UNDER this printed question. The handwritten text describes the patient's job duties and may span 1-3 lines. Common content: physical activities, job responsibilities, equipment used. Capture ALL handwritten text below or to the right of this question until the next printed section begins.

pages.notes: This page contains detailed employment history with two similar job requirement sections - one for the initial job and one for current/different employment. The form includes checkboxes for activity selection at the workplace level, with physical requirements and ergonomic assessments. Contains multiple radio button groups for left/right hand preferences and workspace configurations.

fields.field_label: Reviewed History of Trauma with patient as per the report of Dr.

sections.instructions: This page has two parts side by side. LEFT: A table of body parts with Freq-% and VAS columns. RIGHT: Three numbered free-form writing sections (1, 2, 3) for describing the injury. IMPORTANT: A body part row is 'active' when the patient has circled the body part name OR written values in its Freq-% or VAS cells. Only extract rows where there is a circle around the body part label OR handwritten values in the Freq-% or VAS columns — blank rows should be null. Each row's Freq-% and VAS values belong ONLY to that row's body part. The numbered sections on the right each have a printed number (1) (2) (3) — all handwritten text under each number belongs to that numbered description. At the bottom right is the pre-printed text 'CT: repetitive trauma resulting in orthopedic injuries' — this is static text, not a field. At the top right is '(Patient is a Poor Historian: Yes No)' — the patient or doctor circles one.

fields.position_description: top right, next to the page title
fields.extraction_hints: Printed at the top right of the page: '(Patient is a Poor Historian: Yes No)'. One of the two options is circled. Only the word clearly INSIDE the circle is the answer. Circle can be any color.

Handwritten text in the RIGHT portion of the page, starting at the circled or printed number '1)'. Capture ALL handwritten text between '1)' and '2)'. This describes the first injury event — typically includes a date (e.g. '11-5-21') and what happened. The handwriting may span multiple lines and may be partially illegible. Transcribe as accurately as possible.
Handwritten text in the RIGHT portion of the page, between '2)' and '3)'. Describes additional injury details or a second injury event. May be blank if the patient only filled in section 1.
Handwritten text in the RIGHT portion of the page, below '3)' down to the 'CT: repetitive trauma' line. Describes further injury details or treatment history. May be blank.

tables.extraction_hints: LEFT side of the page — a table with 26 rows (body parts) and 3 columns: body part name, Freq-%, VAS. A row is relevant ONLY if: (a) the body part name is circled, OR (b) there are handwritten numbers in the Freq-% or VAS cells for that row. CRITICAL: Match handwritten values to the CORRECT row by horizontal alignment — the Freq-% value is in the MIDDLE column on the SAME horizontal line as the body part label, and the VAS value is in the RIGHT column on the SAME horizontal line. Do NOT assign values from one row to a different row. VAS may be a single number (e.g. '8') or a range (e.g. '5-6'). Freq-% is a percentage number (e.g. '50', '100'). Rows with no circle and no handwritten values should be reported as empty/null.

fields.field_label: Struck back of head on headrest?

pages.notes: Page contains complex orthopedic injury tracking table with multiple injury categories and scoring systems. MVA section has detailed accident reconstruction questions with multiple selection types. Static text 'CT: repetitive trauma resulting in orthopedic injuries' is informational only, not an input field.

sections.instructions: Page 7 — Pain, stress, and bruxism questions. The first 4 questions are bold-text Yes/No questions — Yes and No appear to the RIGHT of each question. 'Stressors at Work' is a multi-select where the patient circles one or more items from a list across 2 rows. The Clenching/Bracing Yes/No question may have handwritten notes to its right (extract those in p7_clenching_bracing_notes). The bruxism question about Days after / Weeks after has those fill-ins on the SAME line to the right of 'No'. The Bruxism table (Clenching / Grinding / Bracing Facial Musculature) has 3 rows each with 'Day' and 'Night' — the patient circles whichever applies, can circle both Day and Night for the same row. At the bottom: 'Bruxism: ___ % of Time ___ VAS Intensity' — two fill-in numbers on the same line.

fields.field_label: Orthopedic pain causing clenching/bracing of facial muscles
fields.extraction_hints: Bold question at the top: 'Orthopedic pain causing clenching/bracing of facial muscles'. Yes and No appear to the RIGHT of the question text. The patient circles ONE. Only the word clearly INSIDE the circle counts — if a circle touches the adjacent word, that word is NOT selected. Circle can be any color. If neither is circled, return null.

fields.field_label: Developed stressors in response to industrial orthopedic injuries
fields.extraction_hints: Bold question: 'Developed stressors in response to industrial orthopedic injuries'. Yes and No appear to the RIGHT of the question text. The patient circles ONE. Only the word clearly INSIDE the circle counts — if a circle touches the adjacent word, that word is NOT selected. Circle can be any color. If neither is circled, return null.

Label 'Stressors at Work' is bold and printed on the LEFT. The stressor options are printed across 2 rows to the right: Row 1: Heavy Workload, Long or inflexible hours, Tight deadlines, Lack of Control. Row 2: Conflicting or uncertain job expectations, Lack of support, Poor Relationships (with a fill-in line after it). The patient CIRCLES one or more — only circle the items clearly INSIDE a circle. There is also a fill-in line after 'Poor Relationships' for writing in a custom stressor. Do not include items that are not circled even if near a circle.

fields.field_label: Clenching/Bracing of facial muscles in response to stress
fields.extraction_hints: Bold question: 'Clenching/Bracing of facial muscles in response to stress'. Yes and No appear to the RIGHT of the question text. The patient circles ONE. Only the word clearly INSIDE the circle counts — if a circle touches the adjacent word, that word is NOT selected. Circle can be any color. If neither is circled, return null. NOTE: The patient sometimes writes additional notes or context to the RIGHT of Yes/No on this line (e.g. 'Stress from assault', 'when stressed at work as care giver'). Capture those notes in p7_clenching_bracing_notes if present.

fields.field_label: Additional notes next to Clenching/Bracing response
fields.extraction_hints: Handwritten notes written to the RIGHT of the Yes/No on the 'Clenching/Bracing in response to stress' line. Examples: 'Stress from assault', 'when stressed at work as care giver'. If present, transcribe exactly. May be null.

fields.field_label: Did the bruxism begin after the you started having work related pain/stress?
fields.extraction_hints: Bold question: 'Did the bruxism begin after the you started having work related pain/stress?' Yes and No appear to the RIGHT of the question text. The patient circles ONE. Only the word clearly INSIDE the circle counts — if a circle touches the adjacent word, that word is NOT selected. Circle can be any color. If neither is circled, return null. If Yes, there may be handwritten notes to the right. After Yes/No there are also fill-in blanks: '___ Days after ___ Weeks after' — those are separate fields p7_bruxism_days_after and p7_bruxism_weeks_after.

Handwritten number on the same line as the bruxism onset question, after 'No' and before 'Days after'. Only extract if p7_bruxism_after_work_pain is Yes. Sometimes the patient writes a range or approximate (e.g. '~1/2 year' or '90') — transcribe exactly.
Handwritten number after 'Days after' and before 'Weeks after' on the same line. Only extract if p7_bruxism_after_work_pain is Yes. May be blank.

Row 1 of the Bruxism table: 'Clenching'. The patient can circle 'Day', 'Night', both, or neither. This field is TRUE if 'Day' is circled on the Clenching row. Only the word INSIDE a circle counts. Circle can be any color.

Row 1 of the Bruxism table: 'Clenching'. This field is TRUE if 'Night' is circled on the Clenching row. Check the Clenching row specifically — do not confuse with the Grinding or Bracing rows below it.
Row 2 of the Bruxism table: 'Grinding'. TRUE if 'Day' is circled on the Grinding row. The Grinding row is directly BELOW the Clenching row — verify you are on the correct row.
Row 2 of the Bruxism table: 'Grinding'. TRUE if 'Night' is circled on the Grinding row.
Row 3 of the Bruxism table: 'Bracing Facial Musculature'. TRUE if 'Day' is circled on the Bracing Facial Musculature row — the BOTTOM row of the 3.
Row 3 of the Bruxism table: 'Bracing Facial Musculature'. TRUE if 'Night' is circled on the Bracing Facial Musculature row.

Handwritten number on the bottom line before '% of Time'. Format: 'Bruxism: ___ % of Time'. This is on the SAME horizontal line as VAS Intensity — the percent value comes FIRST (left of '% of Time'), the VAS comes SECOND (right of 'VAS Intensity').

Handwritten number after '% of Time' and before 'VAS Intensity' on the bottom line. This is the SECOND number on this line — do not confuse with the % of Time value to the left. VAS range 0-10. Sometimes written as a single digit or with a dash (e.g. '6-7').

sections.instructions: Pre-existing conditions section. Each condition has its own row with a specific layout. ROW LAYOUT for Headaches, Facial Pain, TMJ Pain, Bruxism: The condition label is on the LEFT, then '___ % of time' fill-in in the MIDDLE, then '___ intensity of [condition] on VAS' fill-in to the RIGHT, then a quality/character description line at the far RIGHT. CRITICAL: Match handwritten numbers to the CORRECT row by horizontal alignment — do not assign values from one condition's row to another condition. A circle with a line through it (strikethrough circle) on any row means that value is CANCELLED — return null. The vertex/headache location row has R, L, Forehead, Temple, Occiput, Behind Eyes as circled options. Facial Pain and TMJ Pain rows have R, L, B (both) on the LEFT before the condition label. The nightguard section is below: year made fill-in, 'Still uses? Y N', and reason stopped. Last two questions are standalone Yes/No.

fields.extraction_hints: Row: 'Headaches'. Handwritten value in the blank BEFORE '% of time' on the Headaches row. May be a single number (e.g. '15') or a range (e.g. '10-15'). A strikethrough circle on this field means cancelled — return null.

Row: 'Headaches'. Handwritten number AFTER '% of time' and BEFORE 'intensity of Headache on VAS'. This is the SECOND number on the Headaches row. May be a range (e.g. '7-8'). A strikethrough circle means cancelled — return null.

Row: 'Vertex'. Located BELOW the Headaches row. Options printed in order: R, L, Forehead, then R, L, Temple, then R, L, Occiput, then R, L, Behind Eyes. The patient circles the applicable options. CRITICAL: The R/L before 'Forehead' applies to Forehead, the R/L before 'Temple' applies to Temple, etc. — only include what is clearly circled on THIS row. A strikethrough circle means that item is cancelled.

fields.field_label: Have you ever been diagnosed with migraines?
fields.extraction_hints: Question about migraine diagnosis. Yes and No appear to the RIGHT of the question. The patient circles ONE. Only the word clearly INSIDE the circle counts. A circle with a line/slash/X THROUGH it means CANCELLED — return null. Circle can be any color.
Handwritten details about migraines, to the right of Yes/No. Only if migraine diagnosis is Yes.

Row: 'Facial Pain'. The R, L, B options appear at the VERY LEFT of this row before the label. R = right side, L = left side, B = both sides. Patient circles one or more. Only include options clearly circled ON THIS ROW. A strikethrough circle means cancelled.

Row: 'Facial Pain'. Handwritten value BEFORE '% of time' on the Facial Pain row. May be a range (e.g. '10-15'). This row is BELOW the Vertex row — verify you are on the correct row. A strikethrough circle means cancelled — return null.

fields.field_label: intensity of Facial Pain on VAS
fields.extraction_hints: Row: 'Facial Pain'. Handwritten number AFTER '% of time' and BEFORE 'intensity of Facial Pain on VAS'. Second number on the Facial Pain row. May be a range (e.g. '3-7'). A strikethrough circle means cancelled — return null.

Row: 'TMJ Pain'. R, L, B options at the LEFT of the row before the label. Only include options circled ON THIS ROW — do not confuse with the Facial Pain row above. A strikethrough circle means cancelled.
Row: 'TMJ Pain'. Handwritten value BEFORE '% of time' on the TMJ Pain row. May be blank. A strikethrough circle means cancelled — return null.

fields.field_label: intensity of TMJ Pain on VAS
fields.extraction_hints: Row: 'TMJ Pain'. Handwritten number BEFORE 'intensity of TMJ Pain on VAS'. May be blank. A strikethrough circle means cancelled — return null.

Row: 'Bruxism' (in the pre-existing section, BELOW the TMJ row). Handwritten value BEFORE '% of time' on the Bruxism pre-existing row. A strikethrough circle means cancelled — return null. Note: There is also a Bruxism % field in the section above — this one is specifically for pre-existing Bruxism before the injury.
Row: 'Bruxism' (pre-existing). Handwritten number BEFORE 'intensity of Bruxism on VAS'. May be blank. A strikethrough circle means cancelled — return null.

fields.field_label: If a nightguard was made (year made)

sections.instructions: Surgery section. The label 'Surgery to:' is followed by two rows of body parts. Each body part is preceded by 'R' and 'L' (for Right and Left side). The patient circles EITHER 'R' or 'L' or both to indicate which side was operated on, or circles the body part name itself if side doesn't apply (e.g. 'Back', 'Neck'). The circled letter or body part name is what was selected. CRITICAL: 'R' and 'L' always belong to the NEXT word to their RIGHT on the same row. Example: 'R L Shoulder' — if R is circled, that means 'R Shoulder' (right shoulder). Example: 'R L Elbow' — if L is circled, that means 'L Elbow' (left elbow). Example: 'R Wrist' circled as a group — means right wrist. The doctor may also write a year (e.g. '2023, 2024') ABOVE the circled body part to indicate when the surgery occurred — capture that in p7_surgery_year. A strikethrough circle means CANCELLED — that body part should NOT be included.

fields.extraction_hints: Two-row grid of body parts: Row 1: Neck, R L Shoulder, R L Arm, R L Elbow, R L Wrist, R L Hand, R L Fingers. Row 2: R L Thumb, Back, R L Hip, R L Knee, R L Leg, R L Feet, R L Ankles. For each 'R L [Body Part]' group: the R belongs to the RIGHT side of that body part, the L belongs to the LEFT side. ONLY the circled letter/word counts. Examples: 'R L Shoulder' with R circled → 'R Shoulder'. 'R L Wrist' with both R and Wrist circled as a group → 'R Wrist'. 'Back' circled → 'Back'. A strikethrough circle means CANCELLED — exclude that item. A large X across the whole section means no surgery was performed — return empty list.

Handwritten year(s) written ABOVE the circled body parts (e.g. '2023', '2023, 2024'). Usually written in red ink above the surgery section. May list multiple years separated by commas. If no year is written, return null.

Fill-in line after 'How many times?' at the bottom of the surgery section. May be a number (e.g. '2'), an X mark, or a written word (e.g. '2x'). A strikethrough circle or large X drawn across the line means NOT APPLICABLE — return null. A year written here (e.g. '2024') is the year of surgery, capture in p7_surgery_year instead.

sections.instructions: This section has a single row of treatment options that the patient CIRCLES — they can circle multiple. The options on Row 1 are: Physical Therapy, Chiropractic Manipulations, Acupuncture, Injections, Steroid, Spinal, Trigger Point. The patient circles one or more by drawing a circle around the entire name. Only include a treatment if its name is clearly INSIDE a circle. Below that are two specialist rows: 'Psychological Therapy: Evaluated by ___' and 'Neurologist: Evaluated by ___'. The 'Evaluated by' line may have handwritten text that OVERFLOWS off the printed line and continues below it or to the side — capture ALL connected handwritten text as belonging to that field.

fields.extraction_hints: One row of printed treatment names. The patient circles one or more by drawing a circle around the full name. Only include a treatment if its printed name is clearly INSIDE a circle — if the circle merely touches an adjacent treatment name, that adjacent name is NOT selected. Multiple can be selected. Circle can be any color.

Handwritten text starting on the line after 'Psychological Therapy: Evaluated by'. IMPORTANT: Handwriting may start on the line but then continue BELOW the line or wrap to subsequent lines. Track ALL handwritten text that visually connects to or flows from this line — even if it dips below, curves away, or continues on the next line. The full entry is one connected piece of text. Do not stop at the end of the printed line.

Handwritten text on the line after 'Neurologist: Evaluated by'. IMPORTANT: Handwriting may start on the line but then continue BELOW the line or wrap to subsequent lines. Track ALL handwritten text that visually connects to or flows from this line — even if it dips below, curves away, or continues on the next line. The full entry is one connected piece of text. Do not stop at the end of the printed line.

sections.instructions: This section has the bold centered heading 'Prior History'. Below it are 4 large free-text sections, each with a bold label followed by a colon and a large blank area. The sections are stacked VERTICALLY in this order: 'Past Medical History:', 'Past Surgeries:', 'History of Prior Industrial Injuries:', 'History of Non-Industrial Injuries:'. CRITICAL: Use the bold label as the boundary marker — any handwritten text that appears BELOW a bold label and ABOVE the NEXT bold label belongs exclusively to that label's field. Do not assign text to the wrong field based on vertical proximity. If a section shows the printed word 'None' and no handwriting, return 'None' for that field.

fields.extraction_hints: Bold label 'Past Medical History:' on page 8. Large blank area below it. IMPORTANT: The printed word 'None' appears next to this label. If that word 'None' is CIRCLED (a circle drawn around it), it means there is NOTHING to report — return 'None'. If 'None' is not circled and there is handwritten text, transcribe the handwritten text. If neither is circled and there is no handwriting, the section was left blank — return null. Handwritten text extends until the next bold label 'Past Surgeries:'. Only capture text vertically between those two labels.

Bold label 'Past Surgeries:' on page 8. Large blank area below it. IMPORTANT: The printed word 'None' appears next to this label. If that word 'None' is CIRCLED (a circle drawn around it), it means there is NOTHING to report — return 'None'. If 'None' is not circled and there is handwritten text, transcribe the handwritten text. If neither is circled and there is no handwriting, the section was left blank — return null. Handwritten text extends until 'History of Prior Industrial Injuries:'. Only capture text vertically between those two labels.

Bold label 'History of Prior Industrial Injuries:' on page 8. Large blank area below it. IMPORTANT: The printed word 'None' appears next to this label. If that word 'None' is CIRCLED (a circle drawn around it), it means there is NOTHING to report — return 'None'. If 'None' is not circled and there is handwritten text, transcribe the handwritten text. If neither is circled and there is no handwriting, the section was left blank — return null. Handwritten text extends until 'History of Non-Industrial Injuries:'. Only capture text vertically between those two labels.

Bold label 'History of Non-Industrial Injuries:' on page 8. Large blank area below it. IMPORTANT: The printed word 'None' appears next to this label. If that word 'None' is CIRCLED (a circle drawn around it), it means there is NOTHING to report — return 'None'. If 'None' is not circled and there is handwritten text, transcribe the handwritten text. If neither is circled and there is no handwriting, the section was left blank — return null. Below this label is also a set of 4 MVA fill-in rows (MVA / Injured / No facial-jaw problems / Residual Problems) — those are captured separately in p8_mva_rows. Only capture the free-text handwritten notes between this label and the MVA rows.

Below 'History of Non-Industrial Injuries' there are 4 pre-printed fill-in rows for motor vehicle accidents. Each row has this structure: 'MVA___ Injured___ No facial/Jaw problems or pain   Residual Problems: None___'. For each row: - 'MVA' is followed by a fill-in for the year or date of the accident. - 'Injured' is followed by a fill-in for what was injured (e.g. 'Fractured Pelvis'). - 'No facial/Jaw problems or pain' — if this text is circled, it means no face/jaw issues from this MVA. - 'Residual Problems: None' — fill-in for any lasting problems. If 'None' is circled or written, no residual problems. There may also be handwritten notes ABOVE the rows (e.g. injury description, percentage, VAS) — capture those as part of the relevant row based on vertical position. Only extract rows that have any handwriting — blank rows return null. Summarize all filled rows as a JSON-like string: e.g. 'Row 1: MVA 2018, Injured: Fractured Pelvis, No facial/jaw issues, Residual: R hip pain 30% 8VAS'.

The numbers 0, 1, 2, 3 are in cells to the RIGHT on the SAME row. The patient circles ONE number per row.
